title: Aureline work-item status-transition review packet vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the transition_review_record frozen in docs/work_items/status_transition_review_contract.md. One record family pins the typed review packet a user (or AI assistant pending user confirmation) confirms against before committing a risky work-item transition. The packet pulls together the typed transition trigger (status change, assignee change, provider comment, reopen / close, automation / notification fanout, linked review or change-object update, validation-evidence attach / detach, subscriber change, blocking-relationship change, rename / metadata change, release publish, multi-kind batch), the ordered side-effect fanout rows over the closed five-kind set (provider mutation, local metadata change, linked review update, notification emission, queued follow-on automation), the typed consequence fields per row (target account, authority source, publish mode, undo / rollback posture, offline / deferred handling), the typed automation-followon disclosures, and the typed transition_review_disposition_class so risky transitions cannot silently fan out into provider, notification, or automation side effects under generic 'success' messaging. The packet never claims the provider has accepted the transition; it is the structured what-will-happen review the user confirms against, and the apply path is recorded through the bound status_transition_packet_record (and on apply through the publish-later contract / browser-handoff / offline-handoff lanes). Composes with (and does not replace) the status_transition_packet_record schema (binds back through linked_status_transition_packet_record_id_ref); the change_intent_record schema (binds back through linked_change_intent_record_id_ref) so the rationale / target scope / linked review / approvals stay visible across the review; the external_publish_preview_record schema (binds back through linked_external_publish_preview_record_id_ref) so the previewed before / after labels stay visible; the work_item_detail_record schema (binds back through work_item_detail_record_id_ref); the provider-mode contract (re-exports mutation_mode, irreversibility_class, predicted_side_effect_class); the publish-later contract (cites publish_later_queue_item_record, provider_consequence_preview_record, account_mapping_binding_record on apply); the browser-handoff packet (cites browser_handoff_packet_record on open_in_provider routes); the offline-handoff packet (cites offline_handoff_packet_record on captured-offline routes); the review-workspace / review-pack / change-stack contracts (cites review_workspace_record, review_pack_record, review_evaluation_result_record, change_object_record, patch_stack_record on linked-review-update fanout rows); and the traceability_link_record (cites traceability_link_record on cross-object review rows). Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw label values that disclose customer / tenant identity, raw notification message bodies, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, raw stdout / stderr, and raw automation payloads never cross this boundary regardless of redaction class. The eventual work-items crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new record_kind, transition_trigger_class, side_effect_fanout_kind_class, authority_source_class, publish_mode_class, undo_rollback_posture_class, offline_deferred_handling_class, automation_kind_class, automation_admissibility_class, transition_review_authorization_class, transition_review_disposition_class, denial_reason, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and bumps transition_review_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

transition_review_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the transition-review vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, raw notification payloads, and raw automation payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary safe for support / audit / queue-review / admin-reconciliation surfaces; never carries the raw payload.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in review, queue-review, and export review surfaces. Never carries the raw payload.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the review packet was authored under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class re-exported from ADR-0010 / ADR-0007. Raw payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

provider_actor_class.description: Provider-actor class re-exported verbatim from schemas/work_items/status_transition_packet.schema.json. 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

target_account_class.description: Target-account class re-exported verbatim from schemas/work_items/external_publish_preview.schema.json. Names which connected-provider account / install grant / delegated token / browser-handoff session / managed-admin lane will receive the mutation, or pins local_only_no_provider_account when the row reflects a local-only edit and no provider call will fire.

publish_mode_class.description: Publish-mode class re-exported verbatim from schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json (mutation_mode). The review packet pre-resolves the publish mode per side-effect row so the reviewer sees mutate-vs-draft truth mechanically.

transition_trigger_class.description: Frozen transition-trigger class. Names what the user is about to do at the level the review packet is rendered against. Composes with the underlying status_transition_packet_record's transition_kind_class set; the trigger class is the user-facing review-time grouping (a single transition_review_record MAY carry one packet that aggregates several transition entries). 'multi_kind_batch_trigger' is admissible only when the bound status-transition packet carries entries spanning two or more kind classes.

side_effect_fanout_kind_class.description: Frozen side-effect fanout kind class. Names what the row discloses: provider_mutation_fanout (a typed provider-side mutation will fire on apply); local_metadata_change_fanout (a local-only metadata change will be persisted with no provider call); linked_review_update_fanout (a bound review workspace / review pack / change object / patch stack / branch / worktree row will be updated on apply); notification_emission_fanout (a typed notification will be emitted on apply, or the emission lane is 'unknown until drain'); queued_followon_automation_fanout (a follow-on automation - check run, release workflow, policy evaluation, scheduled task, incident workspace open, notification digest compile, managed-admin review routine - will be queued or fired).

authority_source_class.description: Frozen authority-source class for one side-effect fanout row. Names the truth-source the apply path resolves the row against. provider_authoritative_source is the live provider object; cached_read_only_shadow_source is a read-only cached projection (no provider mutation admissible); imported_evidence_only_source is a snapshot from an offline / object / support / incident handoff (no live provider path); managed_admin_authority_source / policy_admin_authority_source are admin-minted overlays; pending_account_mapping_authority_source is admissible only when the bound account_mapping_binding_record is in pending resolution; local_only_no_provider_authority pins a local-only row with no provider truth.

undo_rollback_posture_class.description: Frozen undo / rollback posture class for one side-effect fanout row. Names the rollback truth the user sees at review time. no_undo_required_local_only is the safe class for local_metadata_change_fanout rows; undo_admissible_within_window pins a soft-reversible window the user can revert within; rollback_admissible_via_compensating_action pins a row that requires firing the inverse provider action (e.g. re-open after close, re-assign after unassign); rollback_admissible_via_revert_state_token pins a row where a typed previous_state_value can be re-applied; rollback_admissible_via_revoke_before_drain pins a deferred / queued / browser-handoff / captured-offline row the user can revoke before drain; rollback_blocked_irreversible / rollback_blocked_release_class pin irreversible side effects (release publish, comment delete, label removal that triggers downstream automation) the user cannot undo locally; rollback_unknown_pending_provider_callback pins a row whose rollback admissibility cannot be predicted before the provider observes the apply; rollback_blocked_imported_evidence_only_no_provider_path pins an imported-evidence-only row with no live provider path to undo against.

offline_deferred_handling_class.description: Frozen offline / deferred handling class for one side-effect fanout row. Pre-resolves the row's deferred-publish lane independently of the packet-level disposition so multi-row reviews can disclose mixed lanes (e.g. local rows resolve not_offline_not_deferred while the provider mutation row resolves deferred_publish_admitted_to_queue). The five typed deferred lanes mirror schemas/work_items/external_publish_preview.schema.json#deferred_publish_class; not_offline_not_deferred is the safe class for publish_now rows.

automation_kind_class.description: Frozen automation-kind class for a queued_followon_automation_fanout row OR for an automation_followon_disclosure entry. no_followon_automation is the safe class for transitions that fire no follow-on automation; the other nine values pre-resolve the typed follow-on the apply path will queue or fire so the reviewer can see automation truth instead of generic 'success' messaging.

automation_admissibility_class.description: Frozen automation-admissibility class for an automation_followon_disclosure entry or queued_followon_automation_fanout row. Pre-resolves whether the follow-on automation will fire admissibly inside Aureline's workspace-trust envelope, will be admitted to the publish-later queue, will route through a browser-handoff packet, or is blocked / unknown. no_followon_automation_in_scope is the safe class for entries that pin no follow-on automation.

transition_review_authorization_class.description: Frozen transition-review authorization class. Pins which actor authored the review packet and under what authorization lane. ai_proposed_pending_user_confirmation forbids admissible_now_publish_now / admissible_via_queue_for_publish_later / admissible_via_browser_handoff_only dispositions until a human actor confirms; imported_evidence_only_not_authoring is admissible only when every side-effect fanout row pins authority_source_class = imported_evidence_only_source and the disposition is admissible_inspect_only_what_if.

transition_review_disposition_class.description: Frozen transition-review disposition class. The packet-level disposition the apply control reads mechanically. The five admissible_* lanes pin the typed apply path; the eight blocked_* lanes pin the typed denial path; withdrawn_before_apply / superseded_by_transition_review_record are terminal lifecycle lanes.

automation_kind_class.description: Required (and outside no_followon_automation) for queued_followon_automation_fanout rows; admissible only as no_followon_automation for non-automation rows.

automation_admissibility_class.description: Required (and outside no_followon_automation_in_scope) for queued_followon_automation_fanout rows; admissible only as no_followon_automation_in_scope for non-automation rows.

linked_artifact_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound upstream artifact (review_workspace_record / review_pack_record / review_evaluation_result_record / change_object_record / patch_stack_record / branch_row / worktree_row / validation evidence record / browser_handoff_packet_record / publish_later_queue_item_record / release_artifact_record / queued automation packet). Required non-empty for linked_review_update_fanout and queued_followon_automation_fanout rows.

linked_traceability_link_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound traceability_link_record (cross-object review). Required non-empty when the linked_review_update_fanout row carries a relation to another work item / review pack / change object / validation evidence row that crosses the work-item boundary.

previous_state_value_label.description: Reviewable label of the prior state (lifecycle / priority / milestone / label / assignee subject ref, etc.). Empty when the row's kind has no prior-state slot.

next_state_value_label.description: Reviewable label of the next state. Empty when the row's kind has no next-state slot.

allOf.description: linked_review_update_fanout rows MUST cite a non-empty linked_artifact_record_id_ref so the bound review workspace / change object / patch stack / branch / worktree / validation evidence is chasable.

queued_followon_automation_fanout rows MUST cite a non-empty linked_artifact_record_id_ref AND resolve automation_kind_class outside no_followon_automation AND resolve automation_admissibility_class outside no_followon_automation_in_scope.
notification_emission_fanout rows MUST resolve notification_side_effect_class outside no_notification_locally_only.

local_metadata_change_fanout rows MUST resolve publish_mode_class to local_draft AND target_account_class to local_only_no_provider_account AND authority_source_class to local_authoritative_source_no_provider_overlay or local_only_no_provider_authority AND notification_side_effect_class to no_notification_locally_only.
provider_mutation_fanout rows MUST resolve target_account_class outside local_only_no_provider_account AND publish_mode_class outside local_draft AND inspect_only.
irreversibility_class irreversible_release_class rows MUST resolve undo_rollback_posture_class to rollback_blocked_release_class.
irreversibility_class irreversible (non-release) rows MUST resolve undo_rollback_posture_class to rollback_blocked_irreversible.
publish_mode_class deferred_publish rows MUST resolve offline_deferred_handling_class outside not_offline_not_deferred AND outside deferred_publish_inspect_only_what_if.
publish_mode_class publish_now rows MUST resolve offline_deferred_handling_class to not_offline_not_deferred.
publish_mode_class inspect_only rows MUST resolve offline_deferred_handling_class to deferred_publish_inspect_only_what_if AND notification_side_effect_class to no_notification_locally_only.
publish_mode_class open_in_provider rows MUST resolve offline_deferred_handling_class to deferred_publish_routed_through_browser_handoff.
imported_evidence_only_source authority MUST resolve undo_rollback_posture_class to rollback_blocked_imported_evidence_only_no_provider_path AND publish_mode_class to inspect_only.
pending_account_mapping_authority_source MUST resolve target_account_class to account_mapping_binding_pending_user_resolution.

side_effect_fanout_row.description: One side-effect fanout row. The packet carries an ordered list of rows so the reviewer sees per-fanout truth (provider mutation row + local metadata row + linked review update row + notification emission row + queued follow-on automation row) instead of a single 'this will publish' summary.

linked_followon_automation_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound queued automation record (publish-later queue item, browser-handoff packet for an automation route, scheduled-task seed, incident workspace packet, etc.). Required non-empty for any automation_kind_class outside no_followon_automation.

allOf.description: Any automation_kind_class outside no_followon_automation MUST cite a non-empty linked_followon_automation_record_id_ref AND resolve automation_admissibility_class outside no_followon_automation_in_scope.
no_followon_automation kind MUST resolve automation_admissibility_class to no_followon_automation_in_scope AND forbid a linked_followon_automation_record_id_ref.

automation_followon_disclosure_entry.description: One automation-followon disclosure entry. Surfaces the typed follow-on automation the apply path will queue or fire so the reviewer can never confirm against a generic 'success' label when an automation will fire downstream. The packet carries an ordered list; an empty list is forbidden (a no_followon_automation entry MUST be present when no follow-on fires, so reviewers see the typed 'no automation' truth instead of an empty surface).

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the review packet was authored under. Reused identically by the work_item_detail / status_transition_packet / change_intent / external_publish_preview schemas.

policy_context.description: Policy context the review packet was authored under. Revalidated at apply; a policy-epoch roll between author and apply re-evaluates the packet.

denial_reason.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary. silent_provider_mutation_under_local_only_review_disposition_forbidden gates a packet from claiming admissible_local_draft_only / admissible_inspect_only_what_if while citing any provider_mutation_fanout row; generic_success_messaging_under_silent_fanout_forbidden is the safety gate that forbids surfaces from rendering an undifferentiated 'success' label when the review packet pins typed fanout rows that the surface MUST disclose.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event vocabulary for the transition-review packet lifecycle.

work_item_detail_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound work_item_detail_record the review packet is rendered against.

linked_status_transition_packet_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound status_transition_packet_record (the previewed mutation the apply path commits). Required non-empty for every disposition outside admissible_inspect_only_what_if and the blocked_* / withdrawn / superseded lanes; an inspect-only review packet MAY exist without a transition packet.

linked_change_intent_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound change_intent_record so the rationale, target scope, linked review, approvals, and validation evidence stay visible across the review. Empty only when the review packet is rendered for a transient transition that has no authored intent (e.g. a managed-admin-minted reopen).

linked_external_publish_preview_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound external_publish_preview_record so the projected before / after labels stay visible. Empty for admissible_local_draft_only and admissible_inspect_only_what_if dispositions that have no provider preview.

linked_publish_later_queue_item_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound publish_later_queue_item_record. Required when transition_review_disposition_class is admissible_via_queue_for_publish_later OR any side_effect_fanout_row resolves offline_deferred_handling_class to deferred_publish_admitted_to_queue / deferred_publish_captured_offline_pending_drain.

linked_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound browser_handoff_packet_record. Required when transition_review_disposition_class is admissible_via_browser_handoff_only OR any side_effect_fanout_row resolves publish_mode_class to open_in_provider.

linked_offline_handoff_packet_record_id_.description: Opaque ref to the bound offline_handoff_packet_record. Required when any side_effect_fanout_row resolves offline_deferred_handling_class to deferred_publish_captured_offline_pending_drain.

linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the bound account_mapping_binding_record. Required when transition_review_disposition_class is blocked_account_mapping_pending OR any side_effect_fanout_row resolves authority_source_class to pending_account_mapping_authority_source.

side_effect_fanout_rows.description: Ordered side-effect fanout rows. Empty array is admissible only for admissible_inspect_only_what_if dispositions that pin no fanout (a pure metadata what-if review).

automation_followon_disclosures.description: Ordered automation-followon disclosures. MUST carry at least one entry; if no follow-on automation fires, the row pins automation_kind_class = no_followon_automation explicitly so reviewers see typed truth instead of an empty surface.

rationale_summary.description: Optional reviewable rationale summary forwarded from the bound change_intent_record so consumers that read the review packet alone (queue-review, support-export) can surface the rationale without re-fetching the change intent.

block_reason_summary.description: Optional reviewable summary of the blocking reason. Required when transition_review_disposition_class is one of the blocked_* lanes; empty otherwise.

supersedes_transition_review_record_id_r.description: Opaque ref to a prior transition_review_record this one supersedes (e.g. after the user re-confirmed under a fresh consequence preview). Empty when this is a fresh review.

expires_at.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp after which the review must not be applied without re-confirmation. The apply path denies an expired review with policy_epoch_expired_re_evaluation_required.

withdrawn_at.description: Required non-empty ISO 8601 timestamp when transition_review_disposition_class is withdrawn_before_apply; empty otherwise.

allOf.description: Every disposition outside admissible_inspect_only_what_if and the blocked_* / withdrawn / superseded lanes MUST cite a non-empty linked_status_transition_packet_record_id_ref so the review packet never floats free of a previewed mutation.
admissible_via_queue_for_publish_later disposition MUST cite a non-empty linked_publish_later_queue_item_record_id_ref.
admissible_via_browser_handoff_only disposition MUST cite a non-empty linked_browser_handoff_packet_ref.
blocked_account_mapping_pending disposition MUST cite a non-empty linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id_ref.

admissible_local_draft_only and admissible_inspect_only_what_if dispositions MUST forbid any provider_mutation_fanout row so a local-only / inspect-only review can never silently fan out into provider state.

admissible_inspect_only_what_if disposition MUST forbid any queued_followon_automation_fanout row whose automation_admissibility_class resolves outside no_followon_automation_in_scope so an inspect-only review can never silently queue follow-on automation.
linked_review_or_change_object_trigger MUST cite at least one side_effect_fanout_row with kind linked_review_update_fanout.
automation_or_notification_fanout_trigger MUST cite at least one side_effect_fanout_row with kind queued_followon_automation_fanout OR notification_emission_fanout.
release_publish_trigger MUST cite at least one side_effect_fanout_row with irreversibility_class irreversible_release_class.
ai_proposed_pending_user_confirmation authorization MUST resolve transition_review_disposition_class to admissible_inspect_only_what_if or admissible_local_draft_only until a human actor confirms.
imported_evidence_only_not_authoring MUST resolve transition_review_disposition_class to admissible_inspect_only_what_if and forbid any provider_mutation_fanout row.

blocked_workspace_trust_unset_or_restricted disposition MUST forbid admissible_via_browser_handoff_only routing on the same packet (a restricted-trust workspace cannot launch a browser-handoff packet).
withdrawn_before_apply lifecycle MUST cite a non-empty withdrawn_at.
superseded_by_transition_review_record lifecycle MUST cite a non-empty supersedes_transition_review_record_id_ref.
blocked_* dispositions MUST cite a non-empty block_reason_summary so a downstream surface can render typed denial copy instead of generic 'something failed' text.

transition_review_record.description: Frozen transition-review record. The packet is the structured what-will-happen review the user, queue-review, and support-export consumers read against; the apply path is recorded through the bound status_transition_packet_record (and on apply through the publish-later contract / browser-handoff / offline-handoff lanes). The packet never claims the provider has accepted the transition.

allOf.description: transition_review_audit_denial_emitted MUST cite a denial reason.

transition_review_audit_event_record.description: Audit-event row for the transition-review packet lifecycle. Denial events MUST cite a typed reason from the closed denial vocabulary; lifecycle events (authored, pinned, re-evaluated, approval added / granted / denied / revoked, committed / admitted / routed / captured / blocked / withdrawn / superseded) carry no denial reason.
